ab: Mun’acang azak tantungu ha’an qudasnanaz takna.
ch: 昨天，我去部落拜訪爺爺。
en: Yesterday, I went to visit my grandpa in the tribe.

ab: Talbuntun azam nii’ tu pasaduu, mantan azak min’asaz cia.
ch: 我們很久沒有見面了，我很想念他。
en: We haven't seen each other for a long time and I miss him a lot.

ab: Saduun zakun at qudas a, musuu’ paka’un zakun sikua, paquu’ zakun savil.
ch: 爺爺一看到我，就請我吃西瓜，請我喝茶。
en: As soon as grandpa saw me, he invited me to eat watermelon and drink tea.

ab: Mantan madavuc ca sikua sincuaz it qudasnanaz, mantan macial kaunun.
ch: 爺爺種的西瓜很甜，真的很好吃。
en: The watermelon grown by grandpa is very sweet and really delicious.

ab: Mazima azak savil it qudasnanaz, macial amin quu’an.
ch: 我也喜歡爺爺的茶，也很好喝。
en: I also like grandpa's tea, it's tasted good too.

ab: Uutu tustusvalin, musku azak it qudasnau’az pitdia paitu qani’an.
ch: 中午的時候，我和奶奶一起煮午餐。
en: At noon, I cooked lunch with my grandma.

ab: Malcikaupata’ ca sinhanat it qudasnau’az a paitu qani’an, maiza ca kaan, tulkuk, bahat, maiza amin ca qazuqazu’.
ch: 奶奶煮了很豐盛的午餐，有魚、有雞、有南瓜、還有蝸牛。
en: Grandma cooked a big lunch, including fish, chicken, pumpkin, and snails.

ab: Mina’unin is paitu qani’an a, munha’an azam pinsanglavan matinguc is sanglav.
ch: 吃完午餐後，我們去菜園摘菜。
en: After lunch, we went to the vegetable garden to pick vegetables.

ab: Madia’ sala ca siniza’ it qudasnau’az a hutan, taii’ ciin calu iscaiv zakun, na ispa’adas zakun mulumaq.
ch: 奶奶拿了很多地瓜、芋頭和蕗蕎給我，要我帶回家。
en: Grandma brought me a lot of sweet potatoes, taro and buckwheat, and asked me to take them home.

ab: Pasumsum azak nai tu mudaan, taqu tu na muqna’ang tantungu nai’an ca ickuin.
ch: 我跟他們道別，說下次再去看他們。
en: I said goodbye to them and said I'd see them next time.
